elements.sent-2411-001: This paper deals with the fractional Sobolev spaces~$W^{s, p (\Omega)$, with~$s\in (0, 1]$ and~$p\in[1,+\infty]$.}
elements.sent-2411-002: Here, we use the interpolation results in~\cite{MR3813967 to provide suitable conditions on the exponents~$s$ and~$p$ so that the spaces~$W^{s, p (\Omega)$ realize a continuous embedding when either~$\Omega=\R^N$ or~$\Omega$ is any open and bounded domain with Lipschitz boundary.}}
elements.sent-2411-003: Our results enhance the classical continuous embedding and, when~$\Omega$ is any open bounded domain with Lipschitz boundary, we also improve the classical compact embeddings.
elements.sent-2411-004: All the results stated here are proved to be optimal.
elements.sent-2411-005: Also, our strategy does not require the use of Besov or other interpolation spaces.
elements.section-2411-001: Introduction and statement of the main results
elements.sent-2411-006: In this paper we prove several results concerning the continuous and compact embeddings of fractional Sobolev spaces~$W^{s, p (\Omega)$, where~$s\in (0, 1]$, $p\in [1, +\infty]$ and \begin{equation}\label{Omega} {either~$\Omega=\R^N$ or~$\Omega\subset\R^N$ is an open and bounded domain with Lipschitz boundary.}\end{equation} All of the embeddings provided here are also shown to be optimal.}
elements.formula-2411-001: \begin{equation}\label{Omega} {either~$\Omega=\R^N$ or~$\Omega\subset\R^N$ is an open and bounded domain with Lipschitz boundary.}\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-007: In particular, our work completes the classification of the fractional Sobolev embeddings by effectively proposing (depending on the interaction between the exponents~$s$, $p$ and~$N$) a series of optimal embedding results.
elements.sent-2411-008: We remark that all the proofs that we present here are self-contained.
elements.sent-2411-009: Moreover, we do not make use of Besov spaces, but rather we use the interpolation results provided by Brezis and Mironescu in~\cite{MR3813967 .}
elements.sent-2411-010: Besides, we point out that the present work refines some of the results on continuous embeddings obtained in~\cite{MR3910033 and~\cite{MR3990737 .}}
elements.sent-2411-011: For this, we provide some specific results of compact embeddings for the spaces~$W^{s, p (\Omega)$ which go beyond the classical ones e.g. in~\cite{MR2944369 and~\cite[Theorem~16.1]{MR350177 .}}}
elements.sent-2411-012: The embedding results that we present here can be of particular interest in the study of problems involving fractional~$p$-Laplacian operators.
elements.sent-2411-013: Indeed, they may enrich the literature and open up new scenarios.
elements.sent-2411-014: To make an example, one could be interested in reconsidering the results in~\cite{TUTTI3 in light of the embeddings presented here and possibly choose some less restrictive assumptions.}
elements.sent-2411-015: To establish our main results, we recall~\eqref{Omega and we introduce the following notation.}
elements.sent-2411-016: For~$s\in [0, 1]$ and~$p\in [1, +\infty]$, we define \begin{equation}[u]_{W^{s,p}(\Omega)}:=  \|u\|_{L^p(\Omega)} if s=0, \displaystyle\left(c_{N,s,p}\iint_{\Omega\times\Omega}\frac{|u(x)-u(y)|^p}{|x-y|^{N+sp}}\,dx\,dy \right)^{1/p} if s\in(0,1), \|\nabla u\|_{L^p(\Omega)} if s=1. \end{equation} Here, $c_{N,s,p}$ denotes a normalizing constant, given explicitly by \begin{equation}c_{N,s,p}:=\frac{s\,2^{2s-1}\,\Gamma\left(\frac{ps+p+N-2}{2}\right)}{\pi^{N/2}\,\Gamma(1-s)},\end{equation} see e.g.~\cite[page~130]{MR3473114 and the references therein.}
elements.formula-2411-002: \begin{equation}[u]_{W^{s,p}(\Omega)}:=  \|u\|_{L^p(\Omega)} if s=0, \displaystyle\left(c_{N,s,p}\iint_{\Omega\times\Omega}\frac{|u(x)-u(y)|^p}{|x-y|^{N+sp}}\,dx\,dy \right)^{1/p} if s\in(0,1), \|\nabla u\|_{L^p(\Omega)} if s=1. \end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-017: The normalizing constant is determined in such a way that \begin{equation}\lim_{s\searrow0}[u]_{W^{s,p}(\Omega)}=[u]_{W^{0,p}(\Omega)}=\|u\|_{L^p(\Omega)} \qquad{and}\qquad\lim_{s\nearrow1}[u]_{W^{s,p}(\Omega)}=[u]_{W^{1,p}(\Omega)}=\|\nabla u\|_{L^p(\Omega)},\end{equation} see~\cite{MR3586796 .}
elements.sent-2411-018: As customary, we define the fractional Sobolev space \begin{equation}W^{s, p}(\Omega):=\left\{u\in L^p(\Omega) such that [u]_{W^{s,p}(\Omega)}<+\infty \right\}\end{equation} endowed with the norm \begin{equation}\label{normaWsp} \|u\|_{W^{s, p}(\Omega)}:= \begin{cases} \|u\|_{L^p(\Omega)} if s=0, \left([u]^p_{W^{s, p}(\Omega)} + \|u\|^p_{L^p(\Omega)}\right)^{\frac1p} if s\in (0, 1), \left(\|\nabla u\|^p_{L^p(\Omega)} + \|u\|^p_{L^p(\Omega)}\right)^{\frac1p} if s=1. \end{cases}\end{equation} We present our main results distinguishing three different cases.
